_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to explain how current technology affects the way we read, create and respond to texts, including media texts.

_source.w: We are learning to explain how current technology affects the way we read, create and respond to texts, including media texts.
y: Technology has fundamentally changed how texts are created, distributed and consumed.
Understanding these changes helps us navigate the digital textual landscape critically.
This awareness makes us more thoughtful creators and consumers of digital content.
eg: We discuss how social media has changed the way news is created, shared and consumed.
We examine how digital tools — like hyperlinks, comments and sharing — change the reading experience.
We analyse how technology enables new text forms — podcasts, vlogs, interactive stories.
We discuss the challenges technology creates — misinformation, shortened attention spans, echo chambers.
We compare reading a physical book with reading on a device and discuss the differences.
We evaluate how digital tools have changed our own reading and writing habits.
